# Combined Strategy: Location + Menu + Operations

## 1. How Location Affects Menu Strategy

The choice of location directly determines the optimal menu strategy. These are not independent decisions.

### Synergy 1: Location-Driven Pricing
- **High-rent downtown location** → Must run high-margin menu with premium pricing
- University area → Price-sensitive customers → Need value items and smaller sizes
- The rent-to-revenue ratio (8–15%) sets a floor on required average ticket size
- **Formula**: Min. average ticket = (Monthly Rent / 30) / (Daily Customers x Margin)

### Synergy 2: Format Follows Location
- Transit hub → Grab-and-go menu (fewer food items, faster service, higher volume)
- Suburban → Full menu with food (customers stay longer, need higher per-visit revenue)
- **Complementary businesses** determine menu: Near gym → protein smoothies; near offices → lunch items

### Conflict: Menu Variety vs. Speed
- High-traffic locations need **fast service** (target: under 3 minutes per order)
- Large menus slow down service (more ingredients, more training, more decisions)
- **Trade-off**: Downtown locations want both high volume AND premium variety — these conflict
- Resolution: Limited core menu + 2–3 daily specials (variety perception without complexity)

## 2. Worked Example

**Scenario:** Downtown corner location, 600 pedestrians/hour during peak

| Metric | Without Optimization | With Combined Strategy |
|---|---|---|
| Monthly Rent | $12,000 | $12,000 |
| Daily Customers | 150 | 200 (+33% from corner visibility) |
| Average Ticket | $5.50 | $7.20 (anchor pricing + upselling) |
| Daily Revenue | $825 | $1,440 |
| Monthly Revenue | $24,750 | $43,200 |
| COGS (25%) | $6,188 | $10,800 |
| Rent % of Revenue | 48.5% (unsustainable!) | 27.8% (still high, improving) |
| Break-even Monthly | $36,000 | $36,000 |

**Key insight**: The location alone does not work without menu optimization — the combined strategy transforms an unsustainable 48.5% rent ratio into a viable (though still aggressive) 27.8%.

## 3. Risks

- **Execution risk**: Combined strategy requires both excellent location AND optimized menu — partial execution may be worse than a simpler approach
- **Seasonality risk**: Downtown foot traffic drops 30–40% during holidays/summer — seasonal menu alone cannot compensate
- **Competition risk**: High-rent corner locations attract competitors — menu differentiation is the moat
